sourceStrategy.coverageNote: Used Google News RSS discovery plus one directly fetched publisher page. Several Reuters and regional-news publisher pages were inaccessible directly, so those retained stories were verified through fetched RSS item metadata.

sources.title: Google to invest up to $40 billion in AI rival Anthropic
keyPoints: Signals a new wave of strategic mega-financing around frontier AI labs.
Raises demand for compute, chips, and enterprise deployment tooling.

sources.title: Canadian AI startup Cohere buys Germany's Aleph Alpha to expand in Europe
keyPoints: Shows European AI consolidation as a way to gain regulatory and market access.
Creates demand for localization, compliance, and sovereign-AI infrastructure.

sources.title: South Korea to invest $166 million in AI chip startup Rebellions
keyPoints: State capital is flowing into local AI chip champions.
Points to policy-backed demand for domestic compute and hardware stacks.

sources.title: The Polsky Center Partners with Harmonic to Expand Data and Insights on Startup Ecosystem
sources.publisher: Polsky Center for Entrepreneurship and Innovation
keyPoints: University-backed ecosystem teams are buying better startup intelligence.
Supports opportunity in founder tooling, market maps, and diligence automation.

sources.title: Government to Designate 10 Startup Cities, Offer 1 Billion Won Prize
keyPoints: South Korea is pairing regulatory relief with place-based startup incentives.
Creates openings for startup formation, incorporation, and local services.

sources.title: New Waterloo quantum startup gains fast momentum with $10.7 million in funding
keyPoints: Deep-tech funding still rewards hard-science wedges with commercialization potential.
Suggests more demand for spinout support and research-to-startup bridges.

signals.title: Frontier AI money is getting bigger and more strategic
signals.summary: Google's planned $40B Anthropic investment, plus South Korea's backing of Rebellions, show that model labs and chip startups are now funded like strategic infrastructure. Best startup angles: compute orchestration, inference cost control, and AI hardware supply-chain tooling.

signals.title: AI expansion is becoming a regional sovereignty play
signals.summary: Cohere's Aleph Alpha deal and South Korea's startup-city incentives point to localized AI ecosystems winning on regulation, data residency, and market access. Best startup angles: compliance automation, sovereign cloud, and cross-border launch services.

signals.title: Startup intelligence and spinout commercialization are getting institutional backing
signals.summary: Polsky's Harmonic partnership and Waterloo's quantum funding show universities and ecosystem builders are buying better data and more commercialization support. Best startup angles: startup-graph tools, diligence software, and research-to-company enablement.

signalMap.title: Yesterday's startup opportunity map
signalMap.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  S1[Capital-intensive AI] --> T1[Strategic funding]
  T1 --> O1[Compute, chips, model ops]
  S2[Regional AI sovereignty] --> T2[Policy + M&A]
  T2 --> O2[Compliance, localization, market entry]
  S3[Ecosystem tooling] --> T3[Data + commercialization]
  T3 --> O3[Startup intelligence, diligence, spinout support]

signalRefs: Frontier AI money is getting bigger and more strategic
AI expansion is becoming a regional sovereignty play
Startup intelligence and spinout commercialization are getting institutional backing

coverageGap: Direct access to several Reuters and regional publisher pages was blocked, so those events were verified via fetched Google News RSS item metadata rather than publisher HTML.
